primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Governs hedge accounting eligibility criteria, specifically paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedge accounting to external party transactions and paragraph 6.3.6 provides the exception for intragroup monetary items

references: The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates
primary_authority.reason: Defines when foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items are not eliminated on consolidation
Specifically addresses hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations and the exception for intragroup monetary items qualifying for hedge accounting

supporting_authority.reason: Governs elimination of intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements and reporting date requirements that affect hedge documentation timing
Defines separate and consolidated financial statements and dividend recognition in separate financial statements

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosures about interests in subsidiaries are topically related but not relevant for determining hedge accounting eligibility
Presentation of financial instruments as liabilities versus equity is not relevant for the hedge accounting question
Distributions of non-cash assets addresses dividend measurement, not hedge accounting eligibility

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items
Standard hedge accounting with external party restriction

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary item foreign exchange risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 explicitly permits hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items where foreign exchange differences are not eliminated on consolidation, provided the hedged risk affects consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: External party transaction restriction applies
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 generally restricts hedge accounting to transactions with external parties in consolidated financial statements, which would exclude intragroup dividends unless the exception applies
